description_long: This is a metallic, single-propeller military airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The aircraft is painted in a reflective silver finish with prominent 'NAVY' markings and insignia, indicating its use by the United States Navy. The wings are straight and feature visible hardpoints or pylons beneath them, likely for mounting ordnance or fuel tanks. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy and positioned towards the front of the fuselage, accommodating a single pilot. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer with a horizontal stabilizer mounted low. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable and currently stowed.
description: A silver, single-propeller Navy airplane with a low-wing design, enclosed cockpit, and military markings. It features a streamlined fuselage and conventional tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, nose, and symmetrical low-mounted wings with 'NAVY' markings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the cockpit canopy, fuselage markings, and detailed wing structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the full span of the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the propeller, cockpit, and additional military insignia on the fuselage and wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles; hardpoints under the wings.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and low-mounted horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, likely stowed).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
